title: Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Business Fees, Charges and Commissions) Order, 2021
title_en: Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Business Fees, Charges and Commissions) Order, 2021

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This Order may be cited as the Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Business Fees, Charges and Commissions) Order, 2021.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Order unless the context otherwise requires: “ Act ” means the Tanzania Shipping Agencies Act; [Cap. 415] “ Board ” means the Board of Directors established under section 21 of the Act ; “ Corporation ” means the Tanzania Shipping Agencies Corporation established under section 4 of the Act ; and “ shipping business services ” means services rendered by the Corporation as prescribed under regulation 4 of the Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Business) Regulations. [ GN No. 386 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: Charging of fees, charges and commission
provisions.content: The Corporation shall, when providing shipping business services in accordance with the Act , charge fees, charges and commissions for the services rendered as prescribed in the Schedule to this Order. [ GN No. 386 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: Revocation and savings
provisions.content: (1) The Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Business Fees) Order, 2019 is hereby revoked. [ GN No. 743 of 2019 ] (2) Notwithstanding the revocation under subparagraph (1), any order, ruling, declaration or finding made or given under the revoked Order shall have the same force or effect as if it had been made or given under this Order. Schedule (Made under order 3) Aproved fees and charges for shipping business services A – Clearing and forwarding agency fees (USD) S/No Tariff item Unit Fee Remarks 1. Container TEU USD 200 or 0.4% of value whichever is higher On account of Shipper or Consignee 2. Container FEU USD 250 or 0.4% of value whichever is higher On account of shipper or Consignee 3. Saloon cars, pickups, station Wagons and Carriers, Trucks, Trailer and buses Unit USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee 4. Heavy Machines-related to Mining activities Unit USD 250 or 0.2% of value whichever is higher On account of Shipper or Consignee 5. Spare parts (Machine/Equipment) BL USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee 6. Liquid Bulk (Petrol) Ltr TZS 3.0 On account of Shipper or Consignee 7. Liquid Bulk (Diesel) Ltr TZS 3.0 On account of Shipper or Consignee 8. Liquid Bulk (Jet fuels: Jet Al, Kerosene and related products) Ltr TZS 2.0 On account of Shipper or Consignee 9. Liquid Bulk (Crude Oil) Ltr TZS 1.0 On account of Shipper or Consignee 10. Petroleum by-products (e.g. Pet coke) MT USD 0.16 On account of Shipper or Consignee 11. Firearms and ammunition BL USD 150 On account of Shipper or Consignee 12. Minerals by-products (e.g. copper slag, clinker, fly ash, dust and particles) MT USD 0.4 On account of Shipper or Consignee 13. Government trophies BL USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee 14. Live Animals – Livestock BL USD 200 On account of Shipper or Consignee 15. Live Animals-Birds including live poultry BL USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee 16. Live Animals-Pets including dogs BL USD 50 On account of Shipper or Consignee 17. Other unlisted animals BL USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee 18. Non-domestic Animal BL USD 70 On account of Shipper or Consignee 19. Unlisted consignments including Parcels BL USD 50 On account of Shipper or Consignee 20. Fertilizer for agricultural purposes MT USD 0.2[Minimum: USD 200] On account of Shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 FEU USD 150 21. Fertilizer for other purposes MT USD 0.5[Minimum: USD 200] On account of Shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 FEU USD 150 22. Industrial Sugar MT USD 0.3[Minimum: USD 200] On account of shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 FEU USD 150 23. Domestic Sugar MT USD 0.2[Minimum: USD 200] On account of Shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 On account of Shipper or Consignee FEU USD 150 24. Edible/Cooking Oil MT USD 0.4[Minimum: USD 200] On account of shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 FEU USD 150 25. Wheat MT USD 0.4[Minimum: USD 200] On account of shipper or Consignee TEU USD 100 FEU USD 150 26. Chemicals-Maritime transport MT Up to 5000MT: USD 0.5>5000MT: USD 0.4[Minimum: USD 300] On account of shipper or Consignee TEU USD 150 FEU USD 200 27. Chemicals-Air transport Value 0.3% of Customs value[Minimum: USD50] On account of Shipper or Consignee 28. Gas-Liquefied MT USD 0.2[Minimum: USD 200] On account of Shipper or Consignee NOTE: Minimum chargeable agency Jee per consignment is USD 50 B – Clearing and forwarding agency fees salt, limestone and magnesium oxide S/No Tariff items Tons Fee Remarks 1. Salt, Limestone and Magnesium Oxide Up to 50 USD 1 On account of Shipper or Consignee 51 -200 USD 50 (plusUSD 0.75 for every ton in excess) 201 -1000 USD 162.5 (plus USD 0.50 for every ton in excess) Over 1,000 USD 562.5 (plus USD 0.40 for every ton in excess) NOTE: Minimum chargeable agency fee per consignment is USD 50 C – Clearing and forwarding agency fees for minerals, mineral extracts and concentrates through maritime ports and border posts — Loose cargo S/No Tariff items Tons Fee Remarks 1. Minerals, MineralExtracts and Concentrates-LooseCargo Up to 300 USD 300 Per BL On account of Shipper or Consignee 301 - 1,500 USD 500 Per BL 1,501 - 3,000 USD 1700 Per BL Over 3,000 USD 2500 Per BL NOTE: Precious Minerals to use rates for airport consignments D – Clearing and forwarding agency fees for minerals, mineral extracts and concentrates through maritime ports and border posts — Containerised S/No Tariff items Unit Fee Remarks 1. Minerals, Mineral Extracts and Concentrates— Containerised TEU USD 300 On account of Shipper or Consignee FEU USD 350 E – Clearing and forwarding agency fees for minerals, mineral extracts and concentrates - Airport consignments S/No Tariff items Value (USD) Fee Remarks 1. Minerals, Mineral Extracts and Concentrates— Airport Consignment Samples USD 50 per AWB On account of Shipper or Consignee Up to 500,000 USD 200 per AWB 500,001-1,000,000 USD 300 per AWB 1,000,001-5,000,000 USD 600 per AWB Above 5,000,000 USD 2000 per AWB F – Processing shipping documents (documentation control) fees S/No Tariff items Unit Fee (USD Remarks 1. Processing Shipping Documents B/L 1.5 per B/L or USD 300 per manifest, whichever is higher On account of shipping Line/Owner G – Ship tallying fees S/No Tariff items Unit Fee (USD) Remarks 1. Container-Loading and Unloading TEU 4 On account of shipping line 2. Container-Loading and Unloading FEU 4 On account of shipping line 3. Container-De-stuffing and Stuffing TEU 7 On account of shipping line (LCL) and shipper/consignee (FCL) 4. Container-De-stuffing and Stuffing FEU 8 On account of shipping line (LCL) and shipper/consignee (FCL) 5. Empty Container-Export/Import TEU 1 On account of shipping line 6. Empty Container-Export/Import FEU 1 On account of shipping line 7. Break bulk Loading/Unloading MT 0.4 On account of ship owner 8. Dry bulk-Loading/Unloading MT 0.4 On account of ship owner 9. Edible Oil in bulk and other bulk liquids other than petroleum products MT 0.18 On account of ship owner 10. Vehicles-Loading and Unloading Unit 1 On account of shipping line H – Local shipping agency fees S/No Tariff items Unit Rates (USD) Remarks 1. Issuance of Bill of Lading B/L 38.14 On account of Shipper 2. Amendment B/L 59.32 On account of Shipper or Consignee I – Minimum agency commission, fees and charges S/No Type of services Minimum commission fees and charges 1. Regular liner including RO-RO ships Exports: (i) 3.5% on gross freight for cargo booked by shipping agent (ii) 2.5% on gross freight for cargo booked by principal Imports: (i) 3.5% on gross freight for cargo booked by shipping agent (ii) 2.5% on gross freight for cargo booked by the principal. 2. Charter or tramp service excluding tankers and cruise ships (i) USD 1,500 for the first three days; thereafter USD 250 per day. (ii) Bulk cargo: 2.5% on gross freight booked by shipping agent. 3. Tankers (i) USD 1,500 for Tankers loading or discharging 5,000MT tons and above per call. (ii) USD 600 for tankers loading or discharging less than 5,000 Metric tons per call. 4. Cruise ships Passenger: USD 1,500 for the three days; thereafter USD 250 per day. 5. Other Supplementary Services(Charges cover agency fees or professional services rendered by agents and exclude running costs) (i) Freight collection: 1% of collected amount. (ii) Container demurrage collection 5% of amount collected. (iii) Car hire: 5% of actual payment. (iv) Boat hire: 5% of actual payment. (v) Crew attendance: (a) Embarking/disembarking: USD 50 per crew. (b) Hospitalization: USD 30 per officer/crew per day. (c) Arranging fumigation of vessel: USD 200 per incident. (d) Attending stowaways and or deserters detained on board or repatriated or deceased personnel USD 100 per person. (vi) Container monitoring USD 10 per box. (vii) Transhipment: USD 15 per box. (viii) Ships calling solely for bunkers or water provision: USD 750 for the first day and thereafter USD 200 per day. NOTE: The Corporation shall be entitled to at least minimum commission fees in accordance with section 45(1) of the Tanzania Shipping Agencies (Shipping Agents) Regulations, GN. No. 339/2018 on account of shipping line as provided hereinabove. J – Other charges applicable S/No Item Unit Rates Remarks 1. Value Added Tax (VAT) Principal Amount 18% Chargeable to taxable value on account of Shipper or Consignee 2. Interest Principal Amount 10% Compounded monthly on delayed payments (including Commission on Disbursement) on account of Shipper or Consignee 3. Commission on Disbursement Cash Amount 2.5% Chargeable for remittances (including VAT returns to TRA for unpaid customers invoice) made on account of Shipper or Consignee History of this document 05 February 2021 this version Published in Gazette of the United Republic of Tanzania 57 Commenced 18 January 2021 Assented to Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
